source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Shallow Water Flow Incident, Garden Banks Block 962

event.top_event: Shallow Water Flow from Low-Pressure Wellhead Housing
actions_taken: Wireline logging performed (Image Behind Casing, Cement Evaluation, Noise/Temperature survey)
Ball valves installed on cement ports
24-hour ROV monitoring of cement ports and seafloor
event.summary: On March 28, 2021, at approximately 1921 hours, personnel observed a steady flow from the Lower Cement Port 1 on the 38-inch Low-Pressure Wellhead Housing during riserless drilling operations with the Transocean Deepwater Poseidon drillship at Garden Banks Block 962. The flow occurred approximately 7 hours after 22-inch casing cement was placed. Analysis revealed the flow originated from above the top of salt in a dirty salt section, migrating through a thin channel in the cement behind the 22-inch casing. Fluid samples contained trace hydrocarbons and high chlorides indicative of seawater. The well was temporarily abandoned on April 25, 2021.
recommendations: Setting an additional casing string, such as a 28-inch casing, just above the dirty salt section would have possibly led to much better isolation of the dirty salt section when running and conducting the cement job on the 22-inch casing

hazards.name: High-pressure hydrocarbon and formation fluids in wellbore
Contaminated salt formation at shallow depth

threats.name: Inadequate cement placement and channeling behind casing
Under-balanced pressure conditions during cement setting
Fluid migration through cement channels to wellhead ports

consequences.name: Loss of containment - shallow water flow from wellhead
Hydrocarbon and formation fluid release to seafloor
Well integrity compromise requiring temporary abandonment

controls.name: Cement job design and execution for 22-inch casing
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to isolate formation fluids
controls.lod_basis: Cement provides primary zonal isolation in wellbore

supporting_text: The results of the logging runs with the cement job results indicated the presence of cement behind the 22-in casing and channeling was observed based on logging interpretations
The Ultrasonic Imager Tool/Cement Bog Log/Variable Density Log result suggested partial coverage and channeling
cement contamination during the cementing of the 22-in casing

controls.name: ROV visual monitoring of wellhead and seafloor
controls.barrier_role: Detection of flow and monitoring of containment status
controls.lod_basis: Real-time visual detection of flow from wellhead ports

supporting_text: The ROV remained in place while waiting for the cement to cure before setting down the full casing weight. However, at approximately 1921hrs, 7 hours after the cement was in place, personnel observed and began to monitor a steady flow from the Lower Cement Port 1 on the LPWHH
The ROV performed wagon wheel surveys of the wellhead and seafloor every 3 hours

controls.name: Wireline logging and cement evaluation
controls.barrier_role: Detection and characterization of cement integrity and flow source
controls.lod_basis: Diagnostic tool to identify cement quality and flow pathways

supporting_text: At 2021hrs, a wireline crew ran in the hole with casing evaluation logging instruments. The wireline logging crew performed an Image Behind Casing inspection and Cement Evaluation
On March 31, 2021, a wireline Noise/Temperature (Noise/Temp) survey was performed to determine where the flow initiated

controls.name: Fluid sampling and laboratory analysis
controls.barrier_role: Characterization of flow composition to assess hazard level
controls.lod_basis: Chemical analysis to determine hydrocarbon content and source

supporting_text: The ROV collected two fluid, litho, samples from shallow flow to analyze as requested by BSEE

On April 2, 2021, Shell received the analysis of the two fluid samples collected by the ROV. The first on-site sample showed no sediment, fluid at 8.0 pounds per gallon, presence of a hydrocarbon odor and segregation and film after settling out

controls.name: Installation of ball valves on cement ports
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of flow from cement ports
controls.lod_basis: Physical isolation of flow pathway at wellhead

supporting_text: April 20, 2021, Shell installed ball valves with the ROV on the Lower Cement Port 1 and the Upper Cement Port 1 of the LPWHH. After a packer was set in the well and pressure tested, the ROV closed the ball vales

controls.name: Structural integrity analysis and well design review
controls.barrier_role: Assessment of well integrity to determine safe operational limits
controls.lod_basis: Engineering analysis to evaluate structural capacity despite cement degradation

supporting_text: Shell conducted a multi-disciplinary study to assess the impact of channeling cement on the integrity of the well. The study indicated that despite the channeling and contamination of the cement behind the 22-in casing due to the shallow water flow, there was no limitations to plans that impact bending or axial capacity
April 13, 2021, Shell submitted to BSEE a Structural Integrity Analysis presentation during a meeting

controls.name: Regulatory oversight and approval process
controls.barrier_role: Independent verification and approval of operational decisions
controls.lod_basis: BSEE regulatory authority to approve or restrict well operations

supporting_text: Shell requested a verbal approval from the BSEE Lafayette District to proceed with well operations as previously approved. BSEE Lafayette District gave verbal approval to continue well operations to run the BOP and riser

BSEE granted Shell approval to drill to the 18-in liner. However, Shell was not allowed to run the 18-in liner until the flow from the cement ports could be analyzed and both BSEE and Shell agree on a path forward

controls.name: Well abandonment decision and execution
controls.barrier_role: Final containment action to prevent further fluid release
controls.lod_basis: Permanent cessation of well operations to eliminate ongoing risk

supporting_text: April 25, 2021, Shell submitted their proposed plan forward to BSEE that included analysis of the Noise/Temp logs of the 22-in casing section. After reviewing the results with BSEE, Shell decided to temporarily abandon the well

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
